name: Influence Map
summary: Map the actors, channels, incentives, and integrity risks around a political decision.
whenToUseIt: Use this when analysing lobbying, stakeholder campaigns, advocacy pushes, or why a policy is moving unexpectedly fast.
steps: List the actors who want the decision to move.
Identify their interests, resources, and preferred outcomes.
Map the channels they can use: departments, ministers, committees, media, coalitions, public pressure.
Mark where timing and access matter most.
Flag the main integrity risks and who is underrepresented.
example: An industry body, a union, a minister's office, a department, and a committee can all sit inside the same influence map on one reform.
